title: Iranian State Railways: Freight and Passenger Traffic, 1953/54-1960/61 (World Bank, 1962)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION, not bulk download. Original document is a 1962 scanned World Bank report (data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1962_economic_development_program.pdf), Chapter 4 (Transport and Communications), Table 6, page 82 (printed page -78-). Every value was verified by rendering the exact page to a high-resolution PNG (pdftoppm -r 200) and reading the table directly off the image. IMPORTANT transcription note: as in the companion Table 5 on the preceding page, the source typewriter used a PERIOD as a thousands separator (not a decimal point) for the '1000 tons', 'Million Ton-Km' and 'Million Passenger-Km' columns whenever the value reached four digits (e.g. printed '1.808' for 1953/54 freight tons means 1,808, and '1.588' for 1958/59 passenger-km means 1,588); the 'Million Passengers' column, by contrast, is a genuine decimal (e.g. 1.71 million passengers), confirmed because (a) its values are naturally in the 1-5 range appropriate for 'millions of passengers' and (b) dividing passenger-km by passengers under this reading yields a stable, plausible average trip length of roughly 230-420 km across all 8 years, whereas the alternative reading would produce a physically nonsensical discontinuity. Values in data.csv are plain numbers with the separator convention resolved per column (integers for tons/ton-km/passenger-km, one decimal place preserved for the passengers column) to avoid ambiguity. No value was interpolated, estimated, or fabricated.
units: freight in 1000 metric tons and million ton-km; passengers in millions and million passenger-km. See data.csv column headers.
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
notes: Source document: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1962_economic_development_program.pdf — Chapter 4 (Transport and Communications), Table 6, page 82 (printed page -78-). Iranian solar calendar years (e.g. 1953/54 = 21 March 1953-20 March 1954). Companion table to wb1962-railways-income-expense-1953-1961 (same institution, same page range, same years). Part of a systematic effort to extract Pahlavi-era (esp. 1941-1962) data from primary-source PDFs already in data/raw/.
